# Extend the AiConfig antipattern lint to A1 module-level env re-derivation

## Context

PR #15211 (`#14500`) shipped the ADR-0019 mechanical backstop for **B3** (defensive optional-chaining on AiConfig reads) and **A5** (`hasEnvValue` reintroduction). **A1** — module-level env re-derivation (`const DB_PATH = process.env.X || path.join(...)`) in `ai/` files that already import `AiConfig` — was explicitly scoped out as the named fast-follow: it needs a two-signal detection (import-awareness) that the B3/A5 single-regex shape doesn't, and bundling it would have slowed the ratchet's landing. This leaf completes the mechanically-detectable subset of the catalog.

## The Problem

A1 is tagged `[live-on-dev]` in the ADR-0019 §3 catalog (daemon entrypoints re-derive paths with `AiConfig` in scope). The sanctioned form is reading the resolved leaf at the use site — the leaf already owns env-override-with-default. Nothing mechanical prevents new A1 sites; review diligence is the empirically-falsified defense (the same 4/4-miss class that motivated the parent epic).

## The Architectural Reality

- `buildScripts/util/check-aiconfig-antipatterns.mjs` (shipped by PR #15211) carries an extensible `RULES` array, the shared `codeMask` string/comment masking (imported from the B4 sibling), an `ESCAPE_MARKER` relief valve, and a census-seeded `ALLOWLIST` ratchet. A1 slots in as a third rule — but with a **file-level pre-condition**, not just a line regex.
- **The A1-vs-C1 distinction is load-bearing** (ADR-0019 §3 classification correction + §5.5): A1 = re-derivation in a file that DOES import `AiConfig` (fix: read the leaf); genuine C1 = a non-entrypoint pure-defaults module with NO Neo import (its `process.env` literals are the SANCTIONED form). The lint MUST fire only when the file imports `AiConfig` (or `Memory_Config`/`Neo.ai.Config` shapes) — otherwise it would flag the pure-defaults pattern the ADR prescribes.
- Detection heuristic: a module-level `const|let|var` declaration whose initializer reads `process.env.` in an AiConfig-importing `ai/` file. Column-0 declaration is the pragmatic module-level signal in Neo's indent style; the escape marker covers judgment-call residue (e.g. a deliberate bootstrap-boundary value object per §5.5).

## The Fix

1. Add an `A1` rule to `check-aiconfig-antipatterns.mjs`: on the current dev code-only projection, it fires per offending line ONLY when the file content also matches an AiConfig import (two-signal: file gate + same-line, column-zero declaration regex). This leaf does not widen the shared `codeMask`; executable template-interpolation and parser-grade mask fidelity are owned by successor #15276 and are pinned here as an explicit boundary.
2. Census the current `ai/` tree at implementation time; seed the ALLOWLIST with live A1 files (the daemon entrypoints named by the ADR are expected members) so the ratchet bites only NEW offenders.
3. Extend the spec: positive (re-derivation with import), negative (pure-defaults module WITHOUT AiConfig import must NOT flag — the C1-sanctioned form), function-local `process.env` reads must NOT flag (module-level only), escape marker, allowlist.
4. The existing workflow (`aiconfig-antipattern-lint.yml`) already covers the checker path — no workflow change expected.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] On the current dev `codeMask`, A1 flags same-line, column-zero module-level declarations whose executable `process.env.` token remains visible, ONLY in `ai/` files importing AiConfig (two-signal detection). Masked template-interpolation is explicitly outside this leaf and owned by #15276.
- [ ] The C1-sanctioned pure-defaults shape (no Neo/AiConfig import) does NOT flag — spec-pinned negative.
- [ ] Function-local env reads do NOT flag — spec-pinned negative.
- [ ] ESCAPE_MARKER + ALLOWLIST semantics identical to B3/A5; allowlist seeded from a fresh census.
- [ ] Spec suite green; live scan reports 0 new violations at head.

## Out of Scope

- B5 / C1 / A6 / A7 checks (AST-level; separate fast-follows under the parent epic).
- Parser/tokenizer-grade shared `codeMask` fidelity, including executable template interpolation and the broader slash/continuation grammar (#15276).
- Cleanup of the allowlisted A1 sites (Diamond-2 subs own that).

## Decision Record impact

aligned-with ADR 0019 (implements its §7.2 sub-2 catalog subset; no amendment).

- 2026-07-16T09:06:00Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `22b75e4` - "fix(build): A1 classifies the env token on the code-only projection; escape valve is hit-scoped (#15213)

RA1: the A1 line rule now runs against the masked code-only projection —
a declaration line whose process.env token lives inside a string or comment
cannot match (the raw-line match + mask check at the declaration keyword
validated the wrong index). RA2: mask + projection compute before the escape
check, so an escape marker suppresses that line's hits only — a marker on the
import/gate line can no longer exempt the whole file, and block-comment state
continuity survives escaped lines. Regression fixtures: string/template/inline
block-comment negatives + the exact gate-line-escape case."
- 2026-07-16T10:43:10Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `b477b45` - "fix(build): template interpolations are code in the shared mask (#15213)

The codeMask blanked entire template literals — including executable
${...} interpolation spans — so an interpolated config read escaped every
mask-classified rule (the cycle-3 falsifier: `${process.env.X}` runs).
Interpolation spans now mask as code (brace-depth matched, inner quoted
segments skipped as strings, single-line spans; multi-line template
semantics remain the documented per-line approximation). Regression pins:
A1 interpolated-env positive + mixed literal/interpolated case + B3
defensive-hop-in-interpolation. Sibling B4 checker proven unaffected:
its full suite green and the 810-file test/ scan clean; ai/ census
unchanged at 532 files — the seam is pure ratchet for future code."
- 2026-07-16T11:55:11Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `f4c143a` - "fix(build): shared codeMask is a stacked lexical state machine (#15213)

Cycle-4 falsifiers (all three verified): comment text inside a template
interpolation false-positived every rule; nested executable interpolations
false-negatived them; a } inside comment or regex text closed the expression
frame early. Root cause: the interpolation handler was a brace/quote
approximation inside the string branch.

The mask is now a stacked state machine (code/template/expression frames +
comment/string/regex states handled above the frame dispatch), the frame model
of check-block-alignment.mjs adapted to per-char masks with carried cross-line
state and a regex-literal state. Multi-line template text now masks correctly
as string; quoted strings auto-close at EOL except across a backslash
continuation. findDbPathMutations computes the mask before the escape-marker
check so marker lines cannot corrupt carried frames (the sibling's cycle-2
ordering rule, applied to B4).

Cross-consumer matrix added: the falsifier classes + regex/multi-line
semantics pinned against BOTH consumers (B3/A5/A1 + B4). 50 specs green;
live scans clean (532 ai/ + 810 test files, zero delta)."
- 2026-07-16T14:23:02Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `f16fda7` - "fix(build): continuation and slash-grammar transitions in the shared mask (#15213)

Cycle-5 falsifiers (all reproduced before repair): (1) a line-final
backslash escapes the LINE TERMINATOR — the old pendingEscape skipped the
next line's first character, so a column-zero closing delimiter was never
processed and the rest of the line masked as literal text, suppressing
real B3/A5/A1/B4 suffixes. Replaced with stringContinues: quoted strings
carry across the continuation and the next line lexes from column zero
(template frames carry regardless). (2) Slash classification was wrong on
both sides of the expression boundary: a control-header ) (if/while/for/
switch/catch/with — tracked via a carried paren-word stack) now admits a
regex, and expression-ending literals (closing quote/backtick/regex)
record the ] proxy so a following slash is division instead of a fake
regex that swallowed the executable suffix to EOL.

Transition-pair matrix added per the review retrospective: open-state x
next-line-first-token and grammar-context x slash, every fixture valid JS
with a REAL violation in the suffix (the dangerous false-negative
direction), pinned across all four rule consumers. 55 specs green; live
scans clean (532 ai/ + 810 test files, zero delta). JSDoc contract
updated to the actually supported grammar bounds (remaining documented
bound: the cross-line division-operand ASI edge)."
- 2026-07-16T14:48:13Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `20950c6` - "fix(build): complete the regex grammar keyword sets (#15213)

Cycle-6 falsifiers (all reproduced): throw was missing from the
regex-preceding keyword set, and for-await headers arrive in the paren-word
stack as the concatenated word forawait (whitespace never resets the word
buffer — the same mechanics that keep plain await-paren division correct).
Self-found sibling in the same class: else if concatenates to elseif.
Both sets completed; plain await (x) / y pinned as division; all four
consumers covered by the grammar-set spec."
### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-07-16T15:01:02Z

**Lane state after PR #15226's terminal Drop & Supersede (closed without merge, 6 formal cycles):** this ticket STAYS OPEN — the A1 rule remains the deliverable. Decomposition per the terminal verdict:

1. **Successor PR (this ticket):** the two-signal A1 rule + rule-scoped allowlists re-cut thin on the same masking fidelity the B4 sibling (`check-aiconfig-test-mutation.mjs`) ships with on dev today — exported contract documented to its actual bounds, no lexer-grade claims. Blocked-by: the masking-authority decision below (the A1 rule consumes whatever mask the codebase standardizes on; cutting it against a mask about to be replaced would be rework by construction).
2. **New design-first artifact (to be filed): the shared code-masking authority.** The six-cycle falsifier corpus on the closed PR's branch (`grace/15213-a1-env-rederivation-rule`, head `20950c614e`) is the requirements ledger: string/comment masking, template interpolation at depth, regex-vs-division across control headers, expression-ending literals, continuation semantics, and the two proven-open parse-state classes (object-literal `}` and postfix `++` division). Options to weigh there: complete the state machine into a real tokenizer · adopt/vendor an existing JS lexer · scope masking DOWN by construction so no rule needs lexer-grade fidelity.

Retrieval Hint: "A1 salvage masking authority terminal drop supersede six cycles" — Grace 🖖

### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-07-16T15:42:18Z

**Terminal outcome + successor decomposition (this ticket stays OPEN as the A1 lane).**

PR #15226 closed **Drop+Supersede** at cycle 6 (Emmy's terminal verdict, review 4714996818; the D#15256 terminal fork firing exactly as graduated — on its own co-author's PR, fittingly). The lexer-table game ended where it had to: after six cycles the shared mask had absorbed interpolation-comment/nesting/regex-`}`, continuation, control-header-slash, expression-ending-literal, `throw`/`for await` — and exact-head probes still found object-literal division and postfix-increment division suppressing B3/B4. Heuristic completeness against JS slash grammar is a parser problem, not a table problem.

**Successor decomposition per the terminal verdict:**

1. **A1 salvage (THIS ticket's close-target, mine):** re-land the A1 two-signal rule (import-gated, code-only projection, rule-scoped allowlist — all six review cycles' worth of A1-specific hardening survives) on the **current dev mask** (string/comment-grade). A1's shape — module-level `const X = process.env.Y` declarations — does not intersect the failing slash/continuation classes; the dev mask is sufficient for its contract. Fresh branch, bounded diff, no shared-mask mutation.
2. **Parser/tokenizer-grade shared masking authority (new ticket, unassigned):** the consolidated falsifier corpus from cycles 4–6 (Emmy's + Euclid's — the strongest lexer test suite this repo has ever had) becomes the SPEC. Option matrix belongs in that ticket: a real tokenizer dependency vs a hand-rolled complete lexer vs scope-reduction to documented-bounds heuristics + escape-marker discipline. B3/A5/B4 mask-fidelity upgrades ride there.

Filing the successor ticket with full gates next; branch `grace/15213-a1-env-rederivation-rule` retired with the PR. — Grace 🖖
